channel.title: Drupal AI
channel.description: Stay informed about developments in Drupal AI.

item.title: #3588588: Add Gemini CLI backend for harness-native plugin install
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.2.1
  • Status: Fixed
  • Discussion: #3588588 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The drupal_devkit tool now includes a Gemini CLI backend for harness-native plugin installation. Previously, Gemini users could only install skills through the marketplace path; there was no way to install harness-native plugins (the equivalent of what Claude Code users get via ClaudeCodePluginBackend). The new backend brings install, uninstall, and list operations to Gemini CLI, closing that gap. Note that only repositories shipping a gemini-extension.json manifest are supported, which is a Gemini CLI requirement rather than a devkit limitation.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New GeminiPluginBackend class in harness_plugins/gemini.py exposes install, uninstall, and list_installed operations for Gemini CLI extensions.

Technical details

GeminiPluginBackend is implemented in src/drupal_devkit/harness_plugins/gemini.py, following the same shell-out pattern as ClaudeCodePluginBackend. It delegates to the gemini extensions install, gemini extensions uninstall, and gemini extensions list CLI subcommands, parses JSON output, performs idempotent installs, and rolls back on failure. Repos without a gemini-extension.json manifest fail at install time with "Configuration file not found" from the Gemini CLI itself. Eleven unit tests in tests/harness_plugins/test_gemini_backend.py cover is_available, install happy path, already-installed detection, install failure, uninstall, list_installed (empty, malformed, and valid JSON with stderr-prefix handling), and info.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588587: Add Codex backend for harness-native plugin install
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Version: 2.2.1
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +810/−0 · py +794 · json +16
  • Diff: 8529e83
  • Discussion: #3588587 · 1 contributor · 5 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The drupal-devkit tool now supports harness-native plugin installation for Codex CLI and Gemini CLI users, not just Claude Code. Before this change, the drupal-devkit harness-plugin install command had no available backend for Codex or Gemini users even though both harnesses were listed in the README as supported. The drupal-workflow marketplace entry is now available under all three harnesses.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: Two new plugin backends registered under type keys "codex-marketplace" and "gemini-extension" in the harness plugin registry

Technical details

The HarnessPluginBackend protocol in harness_plugins/protocol.py already defined the interface (is_available, install, uninstall, list_installed, info). This commit adds two concrete implementations alongside the existing ClaudeCodePluginBackend.

CodexPluginBackend (type "codex-marketplace") manages plugins by combining a CLI call to codex plugin marketplace add to register the upstream marketplace source with direct manipulation of ~/.agents/plugins/marketplace.json, the file Codex reads on startup. Because individual plugin install/list in Codex is TUI-only as of v0.130+, the backend writes plugin entries directly into that JSON file. If _register_plugin raises after a marketplace was added during the same run, the backend attempts a rollback via codex plugin marketplace remove; it correctly skips rollback when the marketplace was already present before the run.

GeminiPluginBackend (type "gemini-extension") delegates entirely to the gemini extensions install/uninstall/list subcommands available in Gemini CLI v0.38+. A notable quirk is that gemini extensions list -o json writes its JSON array to stderr rather than stdout, and may prefix it with non-JSON lines such as theme registration notices. The backend handles this with _parse_json_array, which strips a leading non-JSON prefix before attempting to decode.

Both backends are registered in harness_plugins/__init__.py under their respective type keys in the BACKENDS dict, and corresponding marketplace entries ("drupal-workflow-codex" and "drupal-workflow-gemini") are added to default_marketplaces.json so the registry can resolve them by harness + type.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 14, 2026 (8 days later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588596: Add Webhook support, Project support and events for this
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 3.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 92 files, +6272/−26 · php +5823/−23 · json +238/−1 · txt +108 · yml +103/−2
  • Diff: 0a19cf6
  • Discussion: #3588596 · 3 contributors · 14 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

The gitlab_api module (3.0.x) gains three major capabilities in this release. First, access tokens for GitLab servers are moved from inline config strings to Key module entities, making the Key module a hard dependency. Second, a new GitlabProject config entity lets administrators configure multiple projects, each with its own access token and webhook secrets. Third, a public webhook endpoint at /gitlab-api/webhook/{project} receives GitLab events (issues, comments, push, merge requests, pipelines) and dispatches typed Symfony events, which the eca_gitlab_api submodule exposes as ECA event plugins complete with conditions for slash commands and template validation. Sites upgrading from an earlier 2.x configuration must run drush updatedb and, critically, must switch the auto-created Key entities off the config provider before running drush config:export — otherwise the GitLab access token will be written in plaintext to config YAML and any git commit of that export will expose the secret.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Security: Update hook 9003 creates Key entities with key_provider: config by default; running drush config:export before switching to an environment or file provider will write the GitLab access token in plaintext to config YAML. See UPGRADING.md for remediation steps.
  • Requirements: drupal/key ^1.18 is now a hard runtime dependency of gitlab_api.
  • Database update: New gitlab_seen_event table with primary key on SHA-256 event ID and an index on seen_at for cron-based TTL expiry.
  • Config change: gitlab_server config entity field auth_token (inline string) removed; replaced by auth_token_key (Key entity ID). New gitlab_project config entity type added.
  • Permission change: New administer gitlab_project and use gitlab_api tools permissions, both marked restrict access: true.
  • New API: Nine typed Symfony event classes (GitlabPushEvent, GitlabCommentEvent, etc.), GitlabProject config entity, WebhookController, GitLabApiClientFactory, SignatureVerifier, and ECA event/condition plugins in eca_gitlab_api.

Technical details

The new GitlabProject config entity (gitlab_api.gitlab_project.* schema) holds the GitLab project ID and path, a reference to a parent GitlabServer, an access_token_source field (server-level or per-project Key entity), one or more webhook_secret_keys Key entity IDs, and an optional maintainer username list. The admin UI gains a "Projects" tab alongside "Servers" via new local-tasks and action-links.

The webhook route (/gitlab-api/webhook/{project}, POST, _access: TRUE) is handled by WebhookController::receive, which delegates HMAC verification to the new gitlab_api.signature_verifier service and deduplicates payloads using the gitlab_seen_event database table. Each row stores a SHA-256 hash of the project ID concatenated with the X-Gitlab-Event-UUID header (or a hash of the body when the header is absent). Rows expire after 600 seconds via hook_cron. Nine Symfony event classes are fired on verified receipt: GitlabPushEvent, GitlabTagPushEvent, GitlabIssueCreatedEvent, GitlabIssueUpdatedEvent, GitlabIssueClosedEvent, GitlabIssueReopenedEvent, GitlabCommentEvent, GitlabMrEvent, and GitlabPipelineEvent. All extend GitlabEventBase and carry the full payload and the originating GitlabProject entity.

The eca_gitlab_api submodule maps each event to a derived ECA event plugin via GitlabIntegrationEventDeriver (base plugin eca_gitlab_api_event, derivative IDs :push, :comment, etc.). Plugins expose payload fields as [event:*] tokens and a [gitlab_project] entity token. A project scope filter (all or selected IDs) is implemented through generateWildcard and appliesForWildcard. Two new ECA conditions ship: SlashCommandCondition, which matches /command lines in comment bodies using the stateless SlashCommandParser service and exposes [gitlab_slash:command] and [gitlab_slash:arg:<name>] tokens on match; and BodyFollowsTemplateCondition, which validates minimum character length, ATX Markdown heading count, and required heading titles.

The migration path runs three update hooks: gitlab_api_update_9001 installs the Key module, gitlab_api_update_9002 creates gitlab_seen_event, and gitlab_api_update_9003 reads each server's inline auth_token, creates a Key entity (ID gitlab_api_server_<id>_token, provider config) holding the value, writes the Key ID back as auth_token_key, and clears the inline field. A hook_requirements check surfaces servers where a runtime config override (e.g. $config[...]['auth_token'] in settings.php) outlived the migration, since the override no longer has any effect but still holds the secret on disk.

Upgrade

Run database updates after deploying 3.0.x:

drush updatedb

This installs the Key module, creates the gitlab_seen_event table, and migrates each GitLab server's inline token to a Key entity.

Before running drush config:export, switch every auto-created Key entity off the config provider:

  1. Visit /admin/config/system/keys and edit each gitlab_api_server_<id>_token entity.
  2. Change the Key provider to Environment (or File) and supply the token value via an environment variable or file outside the docroot.
  3. Save, then export config. The exported YAML will contain only the provider configuration, not the token value.

If you previously set auth_token via settings.php or a similar override, that override no longer does anything; remove it. The status report at /admin/reports/status lists any servers where such a stale override is detected.

The two new permissions should be reviewed and granted appropriately:

administer gitlab_project  # restrict access: true
use gitlab_api tools       # restrict access: true — grants write operations to GitLab via Tool plugins

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 12, 2026 (5 days later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • a.dmitriiev (1xINTERNET)
  • jurgenhaas (LakeDrops)

Collaboration

During review, a.dmitriiev observed that the GitLab access token used for a project must have at least the "Developer" role in GitLab; a "Guest"-scoped token returns access denied even with the correct API scopes. a.dmitriiev also recommended that the ECA actions be declared as Tool API plugins so they are available to agents, a suggestion marcus_johansson incorporated before marking the issue ready for review.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588768: [Sprint 2] Replace LLM menu/site-config agents with deterministic scripts
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 7 files, +343/−20 · sh +277 · md +46/−17 · json +17 · js +3/−3
  • Diff: a919b62
  • Discussion: #3588768 · 1 contributor · 6 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Phase 6 of the migrate-site skill previously dispatched two LLM-driven subagents (menu-builder and site-configurator) to fill Drupal admin forms. Because all required data (site name, slogan, front page, logo, menu structure) is already present in migration artifacts, no LLM inference was needed. Recorded cost was roughly 88 minutes wall-clock for a task that scripting can finish in under 5 minutes. Phase 6 now invokes two deterministic bash scripts instead: scripts/configure-site.sh and scripts/create-menus.sh. The LLM agents are retained only as documented emergency fallbacks. Both scripts exit with status 0 always; any item that cannot be processed is appended to docs/migration/blocked.md for manual follow-up.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The SKILL.md Phase 6 section was rewritten to invoke bash scripts/configure-site.sh then bash scripts/create-menus.sh in sequence rather than spawning LLM agent tool calls.

scripts/configure-site.sh reads Site name:, Slogan:, and Front page: headings from docs/migration/plan.md using grep/sed, then calls shared helpers to submit the Acquia site-settings form at /admin/config/system/site-settings. CSS selectors are read at runtime from a new reference file .claude/skills/migrate-site/references/drupal-admin-selectors.json, so a change to the Drupal admin theme requires updating only that one file.

scripts/create-menus.sh reads docs/migration/menu-map.json (a structured JSON file emitted by Phase 1's site-analyzer; schema documented in references/menu-map-schema.md). For each menu it fetches existing items via GET /api/menu_items/<menu> and skips any (label, url) pair already present, making the script idempotent. New items are submitted via the Drupal admin form at /admin/structure/menu/manage/<menu>/add.

Both scripts source scripts/acquia-browser-helpers.sh, which provides three functions: acquia_session_warmup (opens the persistent .canvas-browser/ profile and confirms authentication), acquia_form_post (opens a URL, fills CSS-selector-addressed fields, clicks submit, and returns the final URL), and acquia_classify_redirect (categorizes the post-submit URL as success, sso-waf, or unknown). The success pattern matches /admin/structure/menu/item/<id>/edit; SSO/WAF matches id.acquia.com, source.acquia.com, or a simplesamlphp path. Each script retries once on non-success before writing a structured entry to blocked.md and continuing.

Both scripts support a --dry-run flag. A shell-injection fix in the existing test helper swapped execSync for execFileSync when invoking acquia_classify_redirect from the Vitest suite. Two new test files were added under tests/scripts/: prose-sanity assertions (scripts exist and are executable, SKILL.md Phase 6 references them, menu-builder/site-configurator are demoted to fallback) and unit tests for the redirect classifier covering all three outcome categories.

Upgrade

No upgrade steps are needed for existing Drupal installations. If you maintain a fork of canvas_ai_migrations and customized the Phase 6 agent-dispatch logic in SKILL.md, reconcile against the new script-based invocation:

# Phase 6 now calls scripts, not agents:
bash scripts/configure-site.sh
bash scripts/create-menus.sh

Ensure Phase 1 (site-analyzer) emits docs/migration/menu-map.json in the shape documented in references/menu-map-schema.md (keyed by menu machine name, items with label, url, optional parent and weight). If your admin theme changes Drupal form selectors, update .claude/skills/migrate-site/references/drupal-admin-selectors.json rather than editing the scripts directly.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 11, 2026 (5 days and 2 commits later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588773: [Sprint 2] Replace browser menu automation with menu:administer API (blocked)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +12/−0 · md +12
  • Diff: 2b12f91
  • Discussion: #3588773 · 1 contributor · 6 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The canvas_ai_migrations menu-builder agent (Phase 6) now uses a hybrid approach for menu creation: it reads existing menu state via the Acquia Source OAuth read API before performing any writes, making Phase 6 re-runs idempotent. Writes still go through browser automation against the Drupal admin UI because the upstream menu:administer write endpoints are not yet available. A vitest prose-sanity test suite was added to lock in this documented pattern.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

An empirical probe on 2026-05-01 confirmed that the menu:administer OAuth scope mints valid tokens and GET /api/menu_items/<menu> returns HTTP 200, but POST /api/menu_items/main returns 405 (Allow: GET, HEAD) and POST /api/menu_link_content/menu_link_content returns 404. Write endpoints are not shipped on the current Acquia Source release, so a full API-first replacement of browser automation remains blocked.

The resolution codifies a hybrid pattern in .claude/agents/menu-builder.md under a new HYBRID PATTERN section. Before each write, the agent calls GET /api/menu_items/<menu> and skips any item whose (label, url) tuple is already present in the response. An in-memory set tracks the same tuples within a single Phase 6 dispatch to prevent duplicates even when menu-map.json lists an item under multiple menus. Actual writes still go through scripts/create-menus.sh browser automation against /admin/structure/menu/manage/<menu> (introduced in #3588768). The hybrid does not replace the deterministic script; it adds an idempotency lookup that the script performs before each write.

A companion Vitest file (2b12f91a) adds five prose-sanity assertions that verify the HYBRID PATTERN block is present in menu-builder.md, that all three menu bundle endpoints are named, that the (label, url) deduplication tuple is documented, that browser automation is still referenced for writes, and that the 2026-05-11 verification date is recorded. The Phase 0.5 preflight (Sprint 1 #3588763) is expected to re-probe POST support on each new Acquia Source release; when write endpoints land, the hybrid prose will be rewritten to API-first.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 11, 2026 (5 days and 2 commits later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588762: [Sprint 1] Compaction loses critical facts — propose gotchas.md artifact
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Major feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 2 files, +52/−0 · md +52
  • Diff: d031b00
  • Discussion: #3588762 · 1 contributor · 6 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

Long AI migration sessions that trigger context compaction were causing the agent to lose hard-won technical facts: correct canvas:upload syntax, runtime API quirks, Acquia Platform gotchas. After compaction, the agent would re-discover and re-debug the same issues from scratch. This change introduces a persistent gotchas.md artifact and a formal Session Start preflight protocol so that accumulated knowledge survives compaction boundaries.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New Session Start preflight protocol and Compaction Protocol section in SKILL.md; new versioned template at .claude/skills/migrate-site/references/gotchas.md

Technical details

Two new sections are added to SKILL.md. The Session Start preflight runs before Phase 0 and after every compaction: (1) it copies .claude/skills/migrate-site/references/gotchas.md to docs/migration/gotchas.md if the runtime copy does not yet exist, or preserves the existing runtime copy if the session is resumed; (2) it reads the runtime copy fully into working context; (3) it pre-loads tool schemas via ToolSearch select:<tool_name> so schemas like mcp__chrome-devtools__* are available without reactive re-loading; (4) it runs an orphan safeguard that detects a migration_completed event in state.jsonl without a passing 09-migration-complete-checklist.json and marks the migration RECOVERY_REQUIRED, dispatching artifact-checker with mode: session-start.

The Compaction Protocol section requires that any compaction summary embed the full, verbatim contents of docs/migration/gotchas.md inside a HARD-WON FACTS (preserve verbatim) delimited block. This redundantly preserves the facts in the compaction artifact itself. After compaction, the Session Start procedure is re-executed before the prior phase resumes.

The new template file at .claude/skills/migrate-site/references/gotchas.md ships pre-populated with four sections: Canvas CLI (e.g., canvas:upload runs no validation before upload; canvas:download overwrites local edits silently), Runtime (e.g., getNodePath() returns the truthy string '#'; Tailwind directives only work in global.css), Acquia Platform (e.g., menu JSON:API responses are CDN-cached; media style URLs return thumbnails rather than originals), and OAuth scope vs. endpoint mismatches (e.g., canvas_oauth:menu_link_content mints a token but POST /jsonapi/menu_link_content is 404 on Acquia Source). The runtime copy under docs/migration/ is the per-migration working memory; the skill-dir file is the versioned template. Phase 0.5 (verify-scopes preflight) appends session-discovered mismatches to the runtime copy only.

A Vitest test suite (commit d031b00c) validates that the template file exists with the four required sections and at least one bullet per section, and that SKILL.md contains the Session Start and Compaction Protocol blocks with the expected prose patterns.

Upgrade

No changes to existing site or developer code are required. For maintainers of the canvas_ai_migrations skill: the new session initialization contract means any custom orchestrator that wraps SKILL.md must execute the four Session Start steps before dispatching agents. The runtime artifact layout gains a new file:

docs/migration/gotchas.md   ← runtime working copy, seeded from template
.claude/skills/migrate-site/references/gotchas.md   ← versioned template (do not append here)

When a new gotcha is discovered during a session, append it to docs/migration/gotchas.md (the runtime copy), never to the template.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 11, 2026 (4 days and 2 commits later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3588763: [Sprint 1] Verify-scopes preflight (Phase 0.5)
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Critical feature request
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +341/−0 · js +227 · md +61 · json +53
  • Diff: 0c7b5ee
  • Discussion: #3588763 · 1 contributor · 6 comments
  • Followers: 2

Summary

The canvas_ai_migrations migrate-site skill now runs a "Phase 0.5" preflight check before beginning any migration. It probes every OAuth scope against the Acquia Source instance, classifying each as confirmed, issuance-only, denied, or unknown, and writes results to docs/migration/workflow/scope-availability.json. Downstream agents read this file to decide whether to use an API-first path or fall back to browser automation. The check is non-blocking: a denied or missing scope never aborts the migration. This eliminates the previous failure pattern where a scope issued a valid token but the corresponding API endpoint did not exist on the target Acquia release, causing agents to burn retry turns mid-migration on a 404 that had no resolution.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New classifyResponse(status) export in check-12-verify-scopes.mjs mapping HTTP status codes to one of four scope-classification states (confirmed, issuance-only, denied, unknown)

Technical details

The root cause was that OAuth client_credentials token issuance and API endpoint availability are independent on Acquia Source. A scope like menu:administer could return HTTP 200 from /oauth/token while POST /api/menu_link_content/menu_link_content returned 404 because the endpoint simply did not exist on that Acquia release. Without a preflight, agents discovered this only deep inside Phase 6.

The fix adds scripts/preflight/check-12-verify-scopes.mjs, registered with severity: 'soft' in the preflight runner. On each migrate.sh invocation it reads CANVAS_SITE_URL, CANVAS_CLIENT_ID, and CANVAS_CLIENT_SECRET from .env, then iterates the nine scopes in scripts/preflight/lib/scope-manifest.json. For each scope it requests a client_credentials token, then probes the associated endpoint with the bearer token. The exported classifyResponse() function maps the probe HTTP status to one of four states: confirmed (200/201/204), issuance-only (404/405, meaning the API does not exist on this release), denied (403), or unknown (5xx, network error, or null). Three entries in the manifest are marked _guess for entity types whose JSON:API paths were not verified against a live instance; a runtime 404 from those probes produces issuance-only, which agents already handle by routing to browser automation.

Results are written to docs/migration/workflow/scope-availability.json keyed by scope name with {issuance, endpoint, http_status, probe_url} per entry. The script also appends an "OAuth scopes on this Acquia release" section to the runtime docs/migration/gotchas.md so the orchestrator's context inherits platform state across compaction boundaries. The template copy at .claude/skills/migrate-site/references/gotchas.md is never modified at runtime. Agent instruction files for menu-builder and site-configurator were updated to read scope-availability.json before choosing API vs. browser paths, and SKILL.md gained a Phase 0.5 block documenting the four states and the "log and continue" policy.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: May 6, 2026
  • Committed: May 11, 2026 (4 days and 2 commits later)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3577857: Allow Document Loader to define LLM inputs
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 18 files, +2236/−7 · php +2215/−7 · md +11 · txt +7 · yml +3
  • Diff: 4c5fa3a
  • Discussion: #3577857 · 3 contributors · 11 comments
  • Followers: 4

Summary

Document loader plugins can now declare their own runtime options via a schema method, and those options are automatically surfaced in every integration point: the Document Loader Explorer form, the Field Widget Action plugin, the AI Automator, and the AI tool deriver. Site builders no longer need to create separate input classes or hardcode loader-specific settings. Options marked hide_from_llm_by_default are pre-configured to be hidden from the LLM and locked to their schema defaults on the AI Agent configuration form.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New getLoaderOptionsSchema() method on DocumentLoaderInterface / DocumentLoaderBase allows loader plugins to declare typed runtime options that are automatically rendered in all integration UIs and AI tool schemas.

Technical details

The core addition is getLoaderOptionsSchema() on DocumentLoaderInterface, with a default empty-array implementation in DocumentLoaderBase. Loader plugins override this to return an associative array of option definitions. Each definition carries data_type (string, boolean, integer, list), label, description, required, default_value, optional options (key→label map for select/checkboxes), multiple, and the new hide_from_llm_by_default flag.

To prevent key collisions when multiple loaders declare options with the same name, all options are namespaced as {loader_plugin_id}__{option_key} wherever they cross plugin boundaries. DocumentLoaderBase::extractOwnOptions() strips this prefix at runtime, routing only the current loader's options to it. Colons in derivative plugin IDs (e.g. document_loader:file) are encoded as _C_ in form keys and tool schemas to avoid Drupal context definition parsing issues.

DocumentLoaderTypeFactory::createInput() gains an $extra_options parameter; the factory merges these into the input's options array after type-detection, keeping schema-declared fields separate from loader options. Each integration point (FWA's DocumentLoaderAction, DocumentLoaderAutomatorBase, DocumentLoaderExplorerForm, DocumentLoaderToolDeriver) calls collectLoaderOptionsSchemas() or getLoaderOptionsSchema() to build its form section and then extracts submitted values using the dl__ (automator/FWA) or direct-key (explorer) conventions. The new DocumentLoaderToolHooks class implements hook_form_alter to pre-select "Force value" and "Hide property" on the AI Agent, Modeler API Wrapper, and AI API Explorer forms for any property with hide_from_llm_by_default set.

Upgrade

Loader plugins that want to expose runtime options implement the new method:

public function getLoaderOptionsSchema(): array {
  return [
    'strategy' => [
      'data_type' => 'string',
      'label' => new TranslatableMarkup('Strategy'),
      'description' => new TranslatableMarkup('Processing strategy.'),
      'required' => FALSE,
      'default_value' => 'auto',
      'options' => ['auto' => 'Auto', 'fast' => 'Fast', 'hi_res' => 'Hi-Res'],
      'hide_from_llm_by_default' => FALSE,
    ],
  ];
}

Inside your loader's loadDocument() (or equivalent), call the new extractOwnOptions() helper to retrieve only your loader's options from the input:

$opts = $this->extractOwnOptions($input->getOptions());
$strategy = $opts['strategy'] ?? 'auto';

No changes are needed if you do not need to declare loader-specific options.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 9, 2026
  • Committed: May 6, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • ahmad khader (Vardot)
  • robloach (Kalamuna)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3580850: Integrate with the MDX editor
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 22 files, +2055/−5 · php +1239/−2 · md +456/−3 · js +262 · yml +46 · css +37 · svg +14 · txt +1
  • Diff: 0130d59
  • Discussion: #3580850 · 6 contributors · 29 comments
  • Followers: 7

Summary

The document_loader module now ships two new optional submodules. document_loader_mdx adds a "Load Document" button to the MDX editor toolbar (used by the ai module). Clicking it opens a modal where editors can select a media file from the Drupal media library, enter a URL or Drupal file reference, or supply JSON API options (method, headers, body). The loaded content is sanitized for MDX compatibility and inserted directly into the editor without a page reload. document_loader_media is a companion submodule that provides the reusable media library opener used by the dialog. Requires AI module 1.4 or higher; the install hook checks for compatibility and uninstalls itself if the requirement is not met.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New document_loader_mdx and document_loader_media submodules with FillMdxEditorCommand AJAX command and DocumentLoaderMediaLibraryOpener media library opener service

Technical details

The document_loader_mdx submodule injects a toolbar button using Drupal.behaviors.documentLoaderMdxButton. Because the MDX editor is an async React app, the behavior uses a MutationObserver to detect when the .mdxeditor-toolbar element appears, then inserts a <button> imperatively before the last toolbar child (the diff/source toggle group). A second MutationObserver on the toolbar watches for toolbarTitleMode class changes so the button is hidden in diff/source mode and re-positioned when switching back to rich-text mode.

DocumentLoaderMdxForm is a FormBase modal served at /document-loader/mdx/dialog. It calls DocumentLoaderManager::discoverSourceCategories() to dynamically enumerate all available loaders, filters to those that produce markdown or text output, and renders the active category's fields. For the file category it renders a media library widget backed by a custom document_loader_media.media_library.opener service; this avoids the UploadedFile PHP-serialization problem that would arise from using #type => 'managed_file' in an AJAX modal. For URL inputs, resolveFileUrlInput() inspects the URL path extension against DocumentLoaderManager::collectAllSupportedExtensions() and redirects matching URLs to file_input so they are downloaded as files rather than scraped as web pages. The load is dispatched to DocumentLoaderManager::loadFromData() with caller: 'mdx_editor' (enabled by #3585786). sanitizeForMdx() strips HTML comments, raw HTML tags, and image syntax before the content is returned.

The AJAX response uses FillMdxEditorCommand, which implements CommandInterface::render() returning command: 'documentLoaderFillMdxEditor' and the target textarea name. The matching JS handler in commands.fill_mdx_editor.js sets textarea.value, dispatches input and change events, and dispatches the drupal:mdx-fill CustomEvent that the patched AI module listens for to call editorRef.current.setMarkdown() and visually re-render the editor (see #3581363). The modal itself uses a custom #dl-document-loader-dialog selector rather than #drupal-modal so the nested media library modal does not inadvertently close the parent dialog.

Upgrade

No changes to existing APIs or configuration. Enable the new submodules to opt in:

drush en document_loader_media document_loader_mdx
drush cr

AI module 1.4 or higher is required. The document_loader_mdx_install() hook checks for the drupal:mdx-fill string in the compiled ui/mdxeditor/dist/assets/main.js and uninstalls the module automatically if the requirement is not satisfied.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 23, 2026
  • Committed: May 6, 2026 (1 month later)

Key contributors

  • unqunq (DigitalTrotter)
  • ahmad khader (Vardot)
  • bbruno (1xINTERNET)
  • kristen pol (Itty Bitty Byte)
  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • robloach (Kalamuna)

Collaboration

unqunq opened the initial MR (!31) with a working prototype demonstrated via animated GIF. bbruno reviewed the JS and MDX editor integration and left detailed comments. ahmad khader addressed the review and additionally fixed three independently discovered edge cases: the "Load Document" button remaining visible when switching to diff/source mode (solved with a MutationObserver checking for the toolbarTitleMode class), the button appearing first in the toolbar after switching back to rich-text mode (fixed by re-positioning with toolbar.insertBefore(button, toolbar.lastElementChild)), and the tooltip style not matching native Radix UI tooltips (fixed by a custom DOM tooltip using the same CSS custom properties as the MDX editor's _tooltipContent class). robloach updated the patch against AI 1.4.x and merged.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3575412: Create an Automator for Document Loader
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 20 files, +2507/−53 · php +2326/−53 · md +94 · yml +83 · txt +3 · json +1
  • Diff: 0b49a49
  • Discussion: #3575412 · 5 contributors · 29 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

A new document_loader_automator submodule integrates Document Loader with the AI Automators module, letting site builders automatically extract content from files, media, links, or URL-bearing text fields and write it into a destination field without needing any AI provider. Extraction runs on entity save, cron, or batch. Eight destination field types are supported: string, string_long, text, text_long, text_with_summary, json, json_native, and json_native_binary. For formatted text destination fields, a specific text format can be pinned in the automator's advanced settings, which avoids permission failures when cron runs as the anonymous user.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New document_loader_automator submodule with eight AiAutomatorType plugins; new InputResolverTrait in Drupal\document_loader\Trait\ and DocumentLoaderManager::getAvailableOutputFormats() public method added to the core document_loader module.

Technical details

All eight plugins extend DocumentLoaderAutomatorBase, which extends the AI Automators ExternalBase class. Using ExternalBase suppresses the LLM provider and model configuration fields that standard automators display, so no AI module dependency is needed at runtime. The generate() method iterates each item on the source field, calls resolveInputData() to produce a ['file_input' => ...] or ['url' => ...] dispatch array (dispatching on the source field type: file/image, entity_reference for media, link, or any of the text variants), then passes that to DocumentLoaderManager::loadFromData(). The storeValues() method handles plain fields with a direct entity->set() and formatted text fields (text, text_long, text_with_summary) by appending a format column via either a configured automator_use_text_format value or GeneralHelper::calculateTextFormat().

DocumentLoaderJson overrides generate() to force output_format to 'json' regardless of the configured value, and overrides storeValues() to wrap any content that is not already valid JSON as {"content": "..."}. DocumentLoaderJsonNative and DocumentLoaderJsonNativeBinary extend DocumentLoaderJson unchanged, targeting the json_native and json_native_binary field types respectively.

The extraFormFields() method populates the output format select by calling DocumentLoaderManager::getAvailableOutputFormats(), a new public method that collects output_types from all installed loader plugin definitions and returns a sorted, deduplicated list. This means any newly installed loader plugin that supports a new output type appears automatically in the automator configuration form. Source resolution logic is centralised in InputResolverTrait (in Drupal\document_loader\Trait\), which is shared between DocumentLoaderAutomatorBase and the existing DocumentLoaderAction class, removing previously duplicated inline implementations.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 24, 2026
  • Committed: May 3, 2026 (2 months later)

Key contributors

  • unqunq (DigitalTrotter)
  • ahmad khader (Vardot)
  • robloach (Kalamuna)

Collaboration

The issue was originally closed by unqunq after deciding that a Field Widget Actions integration was a better fit. marcus_johansson reopened it with a concrete argument: multiple contrib modules (Unstructured, ai_simple_pdf_to_text, convertapi) were each writing their own version of the same Document Loader automator, and known production users including a Fortune 500 deployment were already running similar patterns; centralising one canonical implementation in Document Loader would eliminate duplication and simplify the dependency tree for the broader AI module ecosystem. robloach reviewed the resulting MR and identified two gaps: the text format selector and storeValues() format logic covered text_long and text_with_summary but not the base text field type, and raised a config schema question about arbitrary loader option keys. ahmad khader addressed both: added text to all format-aware checks and confirmed that the plugin_config schema's type: ignore sequence made the arbitrary keys safe.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3585786: Add universal pre-load and post-load hooks to DocumentLoaderManager::loadFromInput()
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 12 files, +376/−12 · php +312/−8 · md +63/−4 · yml +1
  • Diff: c39782b
  • Discussion: #3585786 · 4 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The document_loader module now exposes two alter hooks that let other modules intercept every document load centrally, without patching individual consumers. A new optional $caller parameter on loadFromInput() and loadFromData() identifies which consumer triggered the load (MDX editor, FWA field widget action, agent tool, explorer form, Drush, or direct API), so hook implementations can apply conditional logic per consumer.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • API change: New optional string $caller = 'api' parameter added to DocumentLoaderManager::loadFromInput() and loadFromData(); ModuleHandlerInterface added as a new required constructor dependency of DocumentLoaderManager.
  • New API: Two new hooks: hook_document_loader_pre_load_alter (fires before loading, mutable: output_format, max_length, loader) and hook_document_loader_post_load_alter (fires after loading, mutable: content, format, metadata, source, original_bytes, loader_label).

Technical details

The core problem was that DocumentLoaderManager::loadFromInput() is the single entry point for all document loading but provided no extension point for other modules. The fix adds two ModuleHandlerInterface::alter() calls inside loadFromInput(), each split into a mutable first parameter and a read-only second parameter to make the hook contract self-documenting.

hook_document_loader_pre_load_alter(array &$params, array $context) fires after validation and access checks but before $loader->load(). The mutable $params array carries output_format, max_length, and loader; the read-only $context carries document_loader_type, input, and caller.

hook_document_loader_post_load_alter(array &$result_data, array $load_context) fires after the loader runs but before the immutable DocumentLoaderResult is constructed. The mutable $result_data array carries content, format, metadata, source, original_bytes, and loader_label; $load_context is read-only and includes the full load configuration plus caller.

Both loadFromInput() and loadFromData() receive a new optional string $caller = 'api' parameter. Existing consumers pass it as a PHP named argument: DocumentLoaderAction (FWA) passes caller: 'fwa', DocumentLoaderTool passes caller: 'tool', DocumentLoaderCommands passes caller: 'drush', and DocumentLoaderExplorerForm passes caller: 'explorer'.

ModuleHandlerInterface is injected into DocumentLoaderManager as a new last constructor argument and registered via @module_handler in document_loader.services.yml. Full hook documentation lives in the new document_loader.api.php file. Kernel tests in DocumentLoaderHooksTest verify both hooks fire correctly using a document_loader_test fixture module that reads override values from Drupal state.

Upgrade

Existing call sites for loadFromInput() and loadFromData() continue to work without changes because $caller defaults to 'api'. Pass it as a named argument to distinguish your consumer in hook implementations:

$result = $manager->loadFromInput(
  'document_loader_type:pdf',
  $input,
  $account,
  'markdown',
  5000,
  caller: 'my_module',
);

If you have a service that manually constructs or decorates DocumentLoaderManager, add ModuleHandlerInterface as its final constructor argument:

new DocumentLoaderManager(
  $plugin_manager,
  $config_factory,
  $document_loader_plugin_manager,
  $type_factory,
  $file_system,
  $file_input_normalizer,
  $module_handler, // new
);

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 20, 2026
  • Committed: April 22, 2026 (2 days later)

Key contributors

  • ahmad khader (Vardot)
  • robloach (Kalamuna)

Collaboration

robloach manually validated the hooks by building a custom module that modified content, confirmed it worked, then added the kernel test suite (DocumentLoaderHooksTest) to the merge request before marking it RTBC.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3542602: Use an existing entity option for the entity reference automator type
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 81 files, +3983/−196 · md +3251/−182 · css +357/−4 · yml +273/−1 · js +81/−9 · svg +21
  • Discussion: #3542602 · 5 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

The "LLM: Entity reference" AI automator type can now reuse existing entities instead of always creating new ones. When configuring the automator, editors can tick a new "Use existing entity" checkbox beneath each enabled field. If all checked fields match an existing entity in the database, that entity is referenced rather than a duplicate being created. If no match is found, the automator falls back to creating a new entity as before.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The change lives entirely in modules/ai_automators/src/PluginBaseClasses/EntityReference.php, which is the abstract base class for the "LLM: Entity reference" automator plugin.

Form layer. extraFormFields() now renders an automator_entity_field_use_existing_{field} checkbox for every enabled target-bundle field, alongside the existing enable/generate pair. The checkbox is shown only when its corresponding automator_entity_field_enable_{field} checkbox is checked (via #states). The config key is saved with the rest of the automator configuration.

Storage layer. storeValues() was extended to perform an entity lookup before deciding whether to create. For each LLM-generated value set, the method:

  1. Builds an entity query filtered to the configured bundle.
  2. Iterates the generated field values; for any field where automator_config['entity_field_use_existing_{field}'] is non-empty, it adds a condition() to the query and sets a $hasMatchFields flag.
  3. If at least one match field is configured and the query returns results, the found entity IDs are collected and $useExisting is set to true.
  4. When $useExisting is true the existing IDs are appended to $targets; otherwise the original entity-creation path runs unchanged.

The lookup uses AND semantics: all fields marked "use existing" must match simultaneously. This was an explicit design decision raised by marcus_johansson during DrupalCon review; the initial patch used ANY (OR) matching and was reworked to AND before merge.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: August 22, 2025

Key contributors

  • merilainen (Wunder)
  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • anjaliprasannan
  • ahmad khader (Vardot)

Collaboration

During review, marcus_johansson noted (citing a discussion at DrupalCon) that the matching logic must be AND-based: checking two fields should only match an entity when both values are equal, not either one. merilainen, who opened the issue and wrote the original patch, updated the logic accordingly. Later, a PHPStan error was flagged after a rebase; ahmad khader stepped in to fix it, retargeted the branch to 1.4.x at marcus_johansson's request, and brought the merge request to completion.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3575594: Add css to make the mdxeditor slightly taller
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 1 file, +4/−0 · css +4
  • Diff: 70d3319
  • Discussion: #3575594 · 6 contributors · 26 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

The MDXEditor rich-text field in the AI module was too short when empty, making it difficult to work with. A min-height: 240px CSS rule (roughly 8 lines of text) has been added so the editor presents a usable amount of space before any content is entered. This is a visual-only change with no functional impact.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The MDXEditor component rendered its [contenteditable] area with no minimum height, so the editor collapsed to nearly nothing when the field was empty. The fix adds one CSS rule to the AI Core module's MDXEditor stylesheet targeting .mdxeditor .mdxeditor-rich-text-editor [contenteditable] and setting min-height: 240px. The selector scope (.mdxeditor parent plus .mdxeditor-rich-text-editor wrapper) ensures the rule applies only inside the rich-text editing surface and does not affect other contenteditable elements on the page. The change was committed to the 1.x, 1.3.x, 1.4.x, 2.0.x, and 2.x branches.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: February 25, 2026
  • First commit: April 2, 2026 (1 month later)
  • Last commit: April 17, 2026 (14 days and 7 commits later)

Key contributors

  • kristen pol (Itty Bitty Byte)
  • bbruno (1xINTERNET)
  • erichomanchuk (Foster Interactive Inc.)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582856: Add Input Length Limit guardrail plugin against DoW attacks
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +603/−0 · php +582 · yml +20 · txt +1
  • Diff: 9af5bb0
  • Discussion: #3582856 · 3 contributors · 19 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

New input_length_limit guardrail plugin blocks chat requests that exceed a configurable character or token count before they reach the AI provider API. This provides a safety net against denial-of-wallet attacks (consuming expensive API tokens), context stuffing attacks, and overwhelming the model's context window.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New ai.guardrail.settings.input_length_limit config schema with max_length, use_tokens, tokenizer_model, check_all_messages, and violation_message keys.
  • New API: New input_length_limit guardrail plugin blocks chat input exceeding a configurable character or token count.

Technical details

The AI module's guardrail system had no built-in way to limit the length of user input sent to AI providers. The new InputLengthLimit plugin (src/Plugin/AiGuardrail/InputLengthLimit.php) extends AiGuardrailPluginBase and implements processInput() as a pre-generate guardrail.

The plugin only applies to ChatInput. Non-chat input types (e.g. TextToImageInput) receive a PassResult and are skipped. By default, it measures the last user message only. When check_all_messages is enabled, it concatenates all ChatMessage texts (joined with newlines) and measures the total.

Length measurement supports two modes: character-based counting via mb_strlen() (the default), and token-based counting via the ai.tokenizer service with a configurable tokenizer_model (defaults to gpt-4). When the measured length exceeds max_length, the plugin returns a StopResult with a configurable violation message supporting @count, @max, and @unit placeholders. The processOutput() method always returns PassResult since this is an input-only guardrail.

The configuration form uses Drupal #states to show the tokenizer_model field only when use_tokens is checked. A bug in the initial implementation used the wrong form element name selector for this visibility toggle, which was fixed during review.

Config schema was added at ai.guardrail.settings.input_length_limit with five properties: max_length (integer), use_tokens (boolean), tokenizer_model (string), check_all_messages (boolean), and violation_message (string). Unit tests cover character limits, token limits, all-messages mode, last-message-only mode, custom violation messages, multibyte characters, and non-chat input passthrough. Kernel tests verify the full guardrail pipeline using AiGuardrail and AiGuardrailSet config entities with the EchoAI test provider.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 2, 2026
  • First commit: April 9, 2026 (6 days later)
  • Last commit: April 17, 2026 (8 days and 5 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • ahmad khader (Vardot)

Collaboration

During review, ahmad khader found a bug where the tokenizer_model field visibility state used the wrong form element name (use_tokens instead of guardrail_settings[use_tokens]), fixed it, and moved the issue to RTBC. marcus_johansson split a broader guardrail config schema bug discovered during this work into a separate issue #3583785 for backporting.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3582575: Add agent skill and drush generator for Guardrail plugins
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.4.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +723/−0 · md +332 · twig +332 · php +58 · gitignore +1
  • Diff: cc6a52e
  • Discussion: #3582575 · 3 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

The Drupal AI module now ships a Drush code generator and an AI agent skill to help developers create custom guardrail plugins. Before this change, creating a guardrail required knowing the full plugin API: the #[AiGuardrail] attribute, base class hierarchy, four result types, streaming interfaces, and config schema requirements. The new drush generate plugin:ai:guardrail command (alias: ai-guardrail) asks five questions and writes a ready-to-edit plugin stub. The agent skill at .agents/skills/create-guardrail-plugin/SKILL.md gives AI coding assistants (such as Claude or GitHub Copilot) a structured guide to scaffold guardrails from a natural-language description.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New drush generate plugin:ai:guardrail command (alias ai-guardrail) scaffolds a deterministic or non-deterministic AiGuardrail plugin stub in any module.

Technical details

Guardrail plugins (#[AiGuardrail]) run before and after AI generation and return one of four result types from Drupal\ai\Guardrail\Result\: PassResult, StopResult, RewriteInputResult, or RewriteOutputResult. Two distinct plugin patterns exist: deterministic (fixed rules, extends AiGuardrailPluginBase, implements ConfigurableInterface + PluginFormInterface) and non-deterministic (calls an AI provider internally, additionally implements NonDeterministicGuardrailInterface, NonStreamableGuardrailInterface, and ContainerFactoryPluginInterface, uses NeedsAiPluginManagerTrait).

Drush generator (src/Drush/Generators/GuardrailGenerator.php) extends DrupalCodeGenerator\Command\BaseGenerator and is registered via the #[Generator] attribute with name plugin:ai:guardrail and alias ai-guardrail. The generate() method interviews for module machine name, label, plugin ID (auto-derived as snake_case from the label), description, and deterministic/non-deterministic choice, then writes src/Plugin/AiGuardrail/{ClassName}.php from one of two Twig templates at templates/Plugin/_ai-guardrail/. The deterministic template produces processInput() with a ChatInput type check, last-message extraction via end($messages), and @todo markers for custom validation logic, plus full ConfigurableInterface boilerplate. The non-deterministic template adds the NeedsAiPluginManagerTrait, a create() method injecting AiProviderFormHelper via ai.form_helper, provider/model configuration in buildConfigurationForm() using AiProviderFormHelper::generateAiProvidersForm(), and submitConfigurationForm() that extracts and type-casts LLM config keys using CastUtility::typeCast().

Agent skill (.agents/skills/create-guardrail-plugin/SKILL.md) is a structured prompt document consumed by AI coding assistants via the /create-guardrail-plugin slash command. It walks through four steps: determine guardrail type, gather requirements, run drush generate plugin:ai:guardrail, add config schema to config/schema/ai.schema.yml under the key ai.guardrail.settings.{plugin_id}, and optionally generate a kernel test using the EchoAI provider. Config schema guidance was added during review (comment #8) after a reviewer found it missing when testing the skill against the InputLengthLimit guardrail from #3582856.

The #[Generator] attribute sets templatePath to __DIR__ . '/../../../templates/Plugin/_ai-guardrail', which is the actual location of the Twig files. The SKILL.md reference section lists templates/guardrail/deterministic.twig (the older path discussed in review), but the authoritative path used by the generator is templates/Plugin/_ai-guardrail/.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: April 1, 2026
  • First commit: April 8, 2026 (7 days later)
  • Last commit: April 17, 2026 (9 days and 5 commits later)

Collaboration

During review, ahmad khader tested the agent skill by running the exact one-shot prompt from comment #5 against the InputLengthLimit guardrail from #3582856. The generated code passed php -l and phpstan --level=1 with zero errors, but the reviewer found that config schema instructions were missing from the skill. This gap was documented in detail in comment #8 and was then incorporated into the SKILL.md before the issue was fixed. The reviewer also raised a naming convention concern about the Twig template directory (templates/guardrail/ vs templates/Plugin/_ai-guardrail/), and the generator was updated to use the latter path.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3579632: Add ai:logs Drush command for inspecting AI LLM calls from the CLI
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 3 files, +471/−0 · php +468 · md +3
  • Diff: a3ad1c9
  • Discussion: #3579632 · 5 contributors · 22 comments
  • Followers: 6

Summary

The ai_logging module now provides a drush ai:logs command (aliases ail, ai-logs) to inspect AI LLM call logs from the terminal. The command queries ai_log entities and displays prompts, responses, provider/model metadata, and tags. It supports filtering by tag (--tag=ai_agents) or provider (--provider=anthropic), limiting result count (--count=20), and viewing single entries with full text (--id=42). This is useful for debugging AI agents, chatbots, or other AI-powered features without opening the Drupal admin UI. The ai_logging module must be enabled and logging must be turned on at /admin/config/ai/logging/settings.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New drush ai:logs command (aliases ail, ai-logs) for inspecting AI LLM call logs

Technical details

The AiLogCommands Drush command class uses AutowireTrait for dependency injection and injects EntityTypeManagerInterface to query the ai_log entity storage. The command has two modes: list mode (default) returns multiple log entries sorted by created DESC then id DESC, and single-entry mode (--id=N) returns one full entry. List mode truncates prompt and output_text fields to 80 characters using core's Unicode::truncate(). Filtering is implemented via entity query conditions on tags or provider fields. The command returns RowsOfFields, so Drush provides --format=json|yaml|csv|table, --fields, and --filter options automatically. The ValidateModulesEnabled attribute ensures ai_logging is enabled before execution. Ten kernel tests cover list mode, filtering, single-entry lookup, truncation, empty results, and row structure. The initial implementation was rewritten after maintainer feedback to use Drush's built-in formatters instead of custom output logic, reducing the command from approximately 500 lines to 130 lines and dropping custom token estimation and formatting utilities.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 17, 2026
  • Committed: April 3, 2026 (16 days later)

Key contributors

  • ahmad khader (Vardot)
  • ajv009 (FreelyGive)
  • scott_euser (Soapbox)

Collaboration

scott_euser (comment #16) reviewed the initial implementation and noted it was overcomplicated with custom formatters, token counters, and truncation logic, and questioned whether to accept it given the project is minimally maintained. ajv009 (comment #18) rewrote the entire command based on that feedback, replacing all custom output formatting with Drush's built-in RowsOfFields formatter, replacing custom utilities with core functions like Unicode::truncate(), and adding kernel test coverage. The rewrite reduced the code from approximately 500 lines to 130 lines and made the command composable with Drush's standard options. The simplified version was accepted.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3577469: Add possibility to record video of functional testing and to run all functional testing on tags
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 7 files, +976/−22 · md +468 · php +388/−21 · sh +92 · txt +16/−1 · yml +12
  • Diff: d00935c
  • Discussion: #3577469 · 6 contributors · 40 comments
  • Followers: 8

Summary

This is an internal change to the testing infrastructure. FunctionalJavascript tests can now record videos of their execution, making it easier to diagnose test failures. Tests also run selectively in CI: on main branches they skip entirely, on issue branches only tests tagged with the issue number run, and on tagged releases all tests run. This has no effect on sites using the module.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

The issue addressed functional test maintainability and debuggability concerns. The solution added video recording and selective test execution.

The base test class (BaseClassFunctionalJavascriptTests) now supports opt-in video recording. When a test sets $videoRecording = TRUE, Chrome runs non-headless and ffmpeg captures the Selenium container's X display (:99.0) at 25fps with 750k bitrate, saving MP4 files organized by module and test class. The recording lifecycle is managed in setUp() and tearDown(). The implementation uses proc_open() to spawn ffmpeg with x11grab input, and stops gracefully by writing 'q' to stdin (ensuring the MP4 moov atom is finalized) with fallback to SIGTERM/SIGKILL. The getMinkDriverArgs() override strips --headless from Chrome options when recording is enabled.

A new shell script (scripts/filter-functionaljavascript-tests.sh) removes test files before discovery to work around a run-tests.sh limitation where --directory ignores @group filtering. On main branches (matching ^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)?\.x$), all FunctionalJavascript tests are removed. On issue branches, only tests with @group {issue_number} are kept. On tags, all tests run. The script exports HAS_FJS=1/0 so .gitlab-ci.yml can skip ffmpeg installation when no tests will run. The script examines CI_COMMIT_TAG, CI_MERGE_REQUEST_SOURCE_BRANCH_NAME, and CI_COMMIT_BRANCH to determine context, then uses find with vendor/node_modules exclusions and grep -Fq to check for issue number annotations.

Other base class improvements: changed default theme from stark to claro for realistic admin UI testing, moved output paths from sites/default/files/simpletest/ to sites/simpletest/ to match core conventions, added setDefaultProvider() helper to reduce boilerplate, and added strict typing throughout.

The two existing FunctionalJavascript tests were updated to enable video recording, add @group 3577469, use strict typing, and use the new setDefaultProvider() helper. The AutoCompleteTagsTaxonomyTest namespace was corrected to remove an extra "Tests" segment.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 6, 2026
  • Committed: April 1, 2026 (25 days and 5 commits later)

Key contributors

  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)
  • harivansh (OpenSense Labs)
  • b_sharpe (ImageX)
  • ajv009 (FreelyGive)
  • a.dmitriiev (1xINTERNET)
  • hestenet (Drupal Association)

Collaboration

marcus_johansson proposed the approach based on prior Behat experience and implemented the initial video recording. b_sharpe reviewed and requested changes to the filter script: using grep -Fq instead of grep -q to avoid regex interpretation of issue numbers, adding vendor and bower_components exclusions, and changing paths from sites/default/files/simpletest/ to sites/simpletest/ to match core conventions. ajv009 tested the implementation locally with DDEV, Selenium, and ffmpeg, implemented b_sharpe's requested changes, and documented the use of AI assistance in the review process. There was discussion about H.264 licensing concerns, resolved when marcus_johansson confirmed that shipping the encoder (via apt packages) rather than using it is what triggers licensing requirements. a.dmitriiev provided final approval.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3456973: AI integration for Views bulk operations
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 2.0.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 5 files, +581/−0 · php +581
  • Diff: 2e5294d
  • Discussion: #3456973 · 10 contributors · 54 comments
  • Followers: 13

Summary

AI Automators in the ai_automators submodule can now be run in bulk via Views Bulk Operations or any other Drupal action UI. To use this, set the automator's worker type to "Action" when configuring it. The automator will not run during normal entity saves - only when explicitly triggered as an action. If edit_mode is disabled on the automator, entities that already have a value for the target field are skipped, which avoids unnecessary AI API calls on existing content.

Impact

Affects module maintainers and site owners.

  • Config change: New valid value 'action' for the worker_type property on ai_automator config entities; automators with this type are exposed as Drupal actions and do not run during entity save
  • New API: New ActionProcessing process rule plugin (id: 'action') and RunAutomatorAction action plugin (id: 'ai_automators_run') with RunAutomatorActionDeriver in ai_automators

Technical details

Background. ai_automators processes fields on entity save using pluggable worker types (called process rules). Previously there was no way to run an automator on demand against a filtered set of existing entities.

New process rule: ActionProcessing. A new AiAutomatorProcessRule plugin (id: 'action', class ActionProcessing) implements AiAutomatorDirectProcessInterface and returns FALSE from shouldProcessDirectly(). This prevents the automator from firing during entity presave. It lives in src/Plugin/AiAutomatorProcess/ActionProcessing.php.

Derivative action plugins. RunAutomatorAction (id: 'ai_automators_run') extends EntityActionBase and uses RunAutomatorActionDeriver to create one derivative per ai_automator config entity whose worker_type equals 'action'. Each derivative is labeled "AI: [automator label]" and scoped to the automator's entity type. RunAutomatorActionDeriver loads all ai_automator configs and filters by worker_type === 'action' inside getDerivativeDefinitions().

Cache invalidation. AiAutomator::postSave() and AiAutomator::postDelete() call \Drupal::service('plugin.manager.action')->clearCachedDefinitions() so derived action definitions stay in sync with config changes without manual cache clears.

Access and bundle checking. RunAutomatorAction::access() compares the entity's bundle against the automator's target bundle. A mismatch returns AccessResult::forbidden() with the automator config entity as a cacheable dependency. Performing this check in access() rather than execute() means VBO correctly reports bundle-mismatched entities as skipped rather than silently counting them as processed.

edit_mode and recursion prevention. execute() checks whether the target field already has a value and, if edit_mode is off, skips the entity with a status message. After calling generateResponse(), the entity is saved inside a ai_automators_entity_can_save_toggle(FALSE) / finally { ai_automators_entity_can_save_toggle(TRUE) } guard to prevent the automator hook from re-triggering. Errors from generateResponse() are caught and logged without aborting the batch.

Naming decision. The initial implementation named the worker type bulk_action. fago requested renaming it to action because the core action plugin system is general-purpose and not limited to VBO. All class names, plugin IDs, and file names were updated accordingly.

Deferred scope. Processor subforms (allowing per-processor configuration UI) were proposed during review but deferred to a separate issue per fago's recommendation.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: June 25, 2024
  • First commit: March 26, 2026 (1 year later)
  • Last commit: April 1, 2026 (5 days and 11 commits later)

Key contributors

  • petar_basic (drunomics)
  • harivansh (OpenSense Labs)
  • fago (drunomics)
  • ajv009 (FreelyGive)
  • a.dmitriiev (1xINTERNET)

Collaboration

The worker type was initially named bulk_action; fago requested renaming to action because Drupal action plugins are general-purpose and not VBO-specific. The bundle mismatch check was moved from execute() to access() after petar_basic noted that the original placement caused VBO to silently count skipped entities as successfully processed. An initial backport to 1.x broke tests due to structural differences in AiAutomator entity definitions across branches; it was reverted and a proper backport was prepared separately.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3579478: Add LiteLLM guardrails type and sync from proxy
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.3.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 10 files, +1162/−3 · php +1154/−3 · yml +8
  • Diff: 5f5b069
  • Discussion: #3579478 · 4 contributors · 13 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

This feature integrates LiteLLM Proxy's server-side guardrail system with Drupal's AI module. Site administrators can sync guardrails configured in their LiteLLM proxy into Drupal as config entities, then attach them to guardrail sets. When a request is sent, the guardrail names are included in the API payload so the proxy enforces them server-side. This allows sites to use guardrails like PII masking or prompt injection detection without implementing the logic in Drupal.

Impact

Affects module maintainers.

  • New API: New LiteLlmGuardrail plugin (type AiGuardrail) for pass-through guardrails enforced by LiteLLM Proxy

Technical details

LiteLLM Proxy exposes a GET /guardrails/list endpoint that returns guardrails configured in its config.yaml. Each guardrail has a name, type, and enforcement mode (pre_call, post_call, during_call). The proxy enforces guardrails when the request payload includes a guardrails array with the names to apply.

The new LiteLlmGuardrail plugin (AiGuardrail type) is a pass-through plugin. Its processInput() and processOutput() methods always return PassResult because Drupal does not enforce anything itself. The plugin stores only the guardrail_name from its configuration.

The sync flow lives in LiteLlmAiConfigForm::syncGuardrails(). It calls LiteLlmAiClient::guardrails(), which fetches the list from the proxy and extracts the guardrail_name field from each entry. For each name, the sync method generates a config entity ID by lowercasing, replacing non-alphanumeric characters with underscores, prepending litellm_, and truncating to 64 characters. If an entity with that ID exists, it is updated. Otherwise a new ai_guardrail entity is created with plugin type litellm_guardrail.

When LiteLlmAiProvider::chat() receives a ChatInput with a guardrail set, it walks the pre-generate and post-generate guardrails. For each LiteLlmGuardrail instance, it extracts the guardrail_name from the plugin configuration. The names are deduplicated and injected into $this->configuration['guardrails'] before delegating to the parent chat() method (which passes configuration values into the request payload). The key is then removed from configuration so it does not bleed into subsequent calls.

A hook_form_alter() implementation adds a notice to guardrail set forms when LiteLLM guardrails are present, explaining that the pre/post distinction in Drupal is irrelevant because enforcement timing is controlled by the proxy configuration.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 16, 2026
  • Committed: March 31, 2026 (15 days later)

Collaboration

ajv009's review caught three edge cases: entity ID collisions when different guardrail names normalize to the same ID, entity ID length exceeding the 64-character config entity limit, and swallowed errors in LiteLlmAiClient::guardrails() that hid connection failures behind a generic message. The length limit was fixed in the final implementation by truncating IDs to 64 characters. ajv009 also noted this MR adds the first tests to the module. nikro agreed with the findings and welcomed the test coverage.

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.

item.title: #3579681: Canvas AI: Add 'YAML Path Equals' and 'YAML Path Exists' plugins
item.description:
• Project: Drupal AI
  • Type: Normal feature request
  • Version: 1.x-dev
  • Status: Fixed
  • Changes: 6 files, +378/−0 · php +365 · yml +12 · json +1
  • Diff: 2bccd05
  • Discussion: #3579681 · 3 contributors · 15 comments
  • Followers: 5

Summary

Canvas AI agents that generate YAML-formatted tool inputs (like the Page Builder and Template Builder agents) can now be tested with two new validation plugins. YAML Path Exists checks whether a specific path is present in the YAML output. YAML Path Equals checks whether a value at a specific path matches an expected value. Test authors can use these plugins when writing automated tests for Canvas AI agents using the AI Agents Test module. This is a testing-only change with no user-facing effects.

Impact

No upgrade or configuration changes required.

Technical details

Canvas AI agents generate tool parameters in YAML format. The AI Agents Test module provides an AgentsParameterTest plugin system for validating those parameters, but it lacked plugins specifically for YAML validation.

This change adds a new test module canvas_ai_agents_test with two new AgentsParameterTest plugins. Both plugins validate only the component_structure parameter.

The YamlPathExistsTest plugin checks whether one or more dot-notated paths exist in the YAML. The YamlPathEqualsTest plugin checks whether values at specific paths match expected values using path=value syntax. Both plugins support multiple checks separated by newlines and support escaping literal dots in keys with a backslash (e.g., sdc\.canvas_test_sdc\.heading).

A new YamlPathHelper service handles the shared YAML parsing and path traversal logic. It uses Symfony's YAML component to parse the string and walks the parsed array using a regex-split path that preserves escaped dots. The service returns an array indicating whether the path exists, the value if found, and any parse errors.

Both plugins inject the helper service, split the rule value by newlines, and call getValueAtPath() for each path. They record detailed results for each check using the base plugin's createDetailedResultRow() method.

Contribution

Timeline

  • Opened: March 17, 2026
  • Committed: March 27, 2026 (10 days later)

Key contributors

  • akhil babu (QED42)
  • marcus_johansson (FreelyGive)

This content is AI-generated and may contain errors. See Drupal Digests for more.
